rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave To File Verified Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 1. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE VERIFIED AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE OPPOSITION WAS TIMELY FILED BASED ON THE COURT'S ORDER OF MAY 20, 2008 AND C.C.P. SECTION 1005(B) AND (C). THE FACTS ALLEGED DO NOT ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR FINDING THAT VINCENT WEBSTER IS AN INTERESTED PARTY WITHIN THE MEANING OF PROBATE CODE SECTION 1043 (A)(B) AND 8270(C). MOREOVER, ANY CLAIM BY WEBSTER FOR ELDER ABUSE APPEARS TO BE BARRED BY THE TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, C.C.P. SECTION 335.1. FINALLY, THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT CHANGES THE ALLEGATIONS AS TO WHAT CHOYCE ALLEGEDLY READ TO DECEDANT. WHEN THIS FACT IS COUPLED WITH THE DECLARATION OF CALVIN JOHNSON OF MAY 27, 2008, THE COURT FINDS THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO BE A SHAM PLEADING. =(302/PJM/VC)
SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT ARDEN VAN UPP's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 8, 2008 PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN LEACH VS. PHANNA CHEA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defts' Opposition To Phanna Chea'S Application For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANT TURK AUTO PARK, INC.'S Motion Of Opposition To Phanna Chea'S Application For Good Faith Settlement IS DENIED. IT APPEARS ALL THREE PARTIES ARE AT FAULT TO SOME DEGREE. GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S WAGE LOSS OF $29,400 AND MEDICAL LIEN OF $27,000, THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT IS REASONABLE FOR IT REFLECTS THE LIMITS OF CHEA'S INSURANCE. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC BELL DIRECTORY A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ROYAL REFERRAL LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re-Notice) Of Motion For Relief From Default And Default Judgment; To Recall And Set Aside Abstract Of Judgment, Writ Of Execution And Notices Of Levy; And To Release Pending Liens (Ccp 473(B))
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANTS ROYAL REFERRAL, L.L.C. AND EILEEN BURKE'S (Re-Notice) Motion For Relief From Default And Default Judgment; To Recall And Set Aside Abstract Of Judgment, Writ Of Execution And Notices Of Levy; And To Release Pending Liens (C.C.P. 473(B)): THE MOTION OF ROYAL REFERRAL FOR MANDATORY RELIEF UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 473 IS GRANTED. BURKE MAY NOT RELY ON THE MANDATORY PROVISION OF C.C.P. SECTION 473 BECAUSE SHE WAS REPRESENTING HERSELF. SEE ESTHER B. V CITY OF LOS ANGELES (2008) 158 CAL APP 4TH 1093, 1100. THE COURT TREATS MS. BURKE'S MOTION AS ONE FOR DISCRETIONARY RELIEF AND GRANTS THE MOTION ON THE CONDITION THAT DEFENDANTS REMIT TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL THE SUM OF $2,337.50 IN FEES AND COSTS PLUS ANY ADDITIONAL FEES AND COSTS FOR APPEARING AT THE HEARING. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLINE R HOWELL VS. CORINTHIAN COLLEGES, INC. DBA BRYMAN COLLEGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Corinthian Colleges, Inc. Dba Bryman College, Lisa Schewizamello, Diane Hardy, Julie Keller'S Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANTS CORINTHIAN COLLEGES, INC, D.B.A. BRYMAN COLLEGE, LISA SCHEWIZAMELLO, DIANE HARDY AND JULIE KELLER'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE APPLICABILITY OF THE COMMERICAL RULES WAS ADDRESSED IN CASE NUMBER 464469 AS WAS PLAINTIFF'S STATUS AS AN EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. THE COURT ORDERED THE MATTER TO ARBITRATION. PLAINTIFF DID NOT CHALLENGE THE RULING BUT DISMISSED THE COMPLAINT. NOW PLAINTIFF FILES AN ACTION ALLEGING SHE IS AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AND ASSERTS THE A.A.A. RULES OF COMMERICIAL ARBITRATION ARE PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF IS COLLATERALLY ESTOPPED TO RE-LITIGATE THIS ISSUE. THE COURT ALSO FINDS THAT THE A.A.A. RULES ARE NOT SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE ARBITRATOR HAS DISCRETION TO ORDER ALL FORMS OF DISCOVERY. BY STRIKING THE LAST TWO SENTENCES OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE, OTHER OBJECTIONS ARE CURED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL L. BALSAM VS. STARTUP CONSULTANTS LLC A FLORIDA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 10. PLAINTIFF DANIEL BALSAM'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FOURTH DEFENSE. SUSTAIN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO NINTH DEFENSE FOR THERE IS NO REQUIREMENT TO ATTACH THE E-MAILS. SUSTAIN WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTEENTH DEFENSE TO PLEAD FACTS TO SUPPORT THE CLAIM. OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINDER OF THE DEFENSES THAT RAISE ISSUES OF FACT OR SIMPLY EXPAND UPON DEFENDANT'S DENIALS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PEGGY SNYDER VS. XTRONX, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Xtronx, Inc., Xtronx T2 Llc And Eric Wong'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 15. DEFENDANTS XTRONX, INC., XTRONX T2, L.L.C. AND ERIC WONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE STANDING AS REQUIRED BY BOHN V SMITH (1967) 252 CAL APP 2D 678, 682. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED PERFORMANCE OF THE CONTRACT, WONG'S AGENCY, AND THE CONTRACT CLAIM IS NOT UNCERTAIN. PLAINTIFF'S ALTER EGO CLAIM REQUIRES ADDITIONAL FACTS TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR THE CONTENTION OF UNITY OF OWNERSHIP. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: COX CALIFORNIA TELECOM LLC (U5684-C) VS. GLOBAL NAPS CALIFORNIA INC (U-6449-C)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Pl'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 11. PETITIONER COX CALIFORNIA TELECOM, L.L.C. (U5684-C) Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award IS CONFIRMED. IS IT UNDISPUTED THAT THE PARTIES HAD A HEARING BEFORE THE C.P.U.C., A THIRD PARTY DECISION MAKER. THE STATEMENTS OF THE C.P.U.C ARE THE BEST EVIDENCE AS TO WHAT THE PROCEEDING WAS. THE C.P.U.C. CONSISTENTLY REFERRED TO THE PROCEEDING AS AN ARBITRATION. GIVEN THAT THE C.P.U.C. ALSO HAS ADJUDICATING AND RULE-MAKING POWERS, ITS DESCRIPTION OF HOLDING AN ARBITRATION IS PERSUASIVE AUTHORITY AS TO THE NATURE OF THE PROCEEDING. THE COURT DECLINES TO STAY THE AWARD. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 12. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Strike The Claim Of Sandra Kay Nieman IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: BROADWAY PROPERTIES LLC VS. WE PARK, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANT WE PARK, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER ON THE MOTION TO QUASH DID NOT PRECLUDE THIS DEMURRER AND THE WRITTEN ORDER IS IN ERROR TO THE EXTENT IT SUGGESTS OTHERWISE. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE LEASE AND OPERATING AGREEMENT BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S REPRESENTATION THAT THE AGREEMENTS ARE TRUE AND CORRECT IN RESPONSE TO THE MOTION TO STRIKE. THE COURT ALSO CONCLUDES THAT POWELL PARTNERS IS AN INDISPENSABLE PARTY BECAUSE IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THIS ACTION HAS BEEN COMMENCED FOR THE MUTUAL BENEFIT OF BOTH LESSORS. SEE LOS ANGELES LIGHTING CO. V LOS ANGELES (1895) 106 CAL 156, 160; B & B SULPHER V KELLY (1943) 61 CAL APP 2D, 11. THE COMPLAINT ALSO FAILS TO ALLEGE WHETHER DEFENDANT'S HAVE FAILED TO CURE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO THE SUR-REPLY IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/X-JL)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD MURPHY VS. DENTA TADESSE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 11, 2008 LINE 14. DEFENDANT DENTA TADESSE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE LEASE IS UNCLEAR AS TO WHAT WAS PAID, WHEN IT WAS PAID AND WHAT WAS TO BE PAID AFTER FOUR MONTHS. =(302/PJM/AY)
